title: Welcome to DDM
description: A taster. A preview. An overview of SlideHeroes' flagship presentations course - Decks for Decision Makers

title: Before we begin...
description: A three question survey to help me understand your goals so I can better help you achieve them

title: Presentation Tools & Course Resources
description: Links to some recommended presentation tools + all course materials and downloads

children.text: This page includes links to all course material and downloads.
I have also included a table listing my current recommendations of the web's best tools, reference sites, and apps for helping create great presentations.
Let me know if I have missed any!
Be sure to bookmark this page for your reference and convenience.
SlideHeroes Business Presentation PowerPoint Template: Over 1,000 slide templates
Lesson slides: pdfs of slides used for each lesson
Golden Rules Pack: a pdf summary of the SlideHeroes Golden Rules

title: Our Process
description: Our blueprint for creating high quality presentations

title: The Who
description: Where do we start? We start with defining who our actual audience is

children.text: Download and complete Audience Map tenmplate
HBR Guide to Persuasive Presentations by Nancy Duarte
Select a presentation that you would like to create as your course project
Identify the audience for your presentation
Create an audience map for your audience

title: The Why: Building the Introduction
description: How to tee-up your presentation

children.text: Identify what your audience already knows; build the context portion of your Introduction
Determine the catalyst for the presentation. What has happened that has created the need for this presentation?
Determine what the question is that you are answering

title: The Why: Next Steps
description: What do we want to accomplish from our presentation? What is our ultimate objective?

children.text: Write down a single goal or next step for yuour presentation
Write down a single goal or next step for yuour presentation
Think about the component pieces of your goal - start thinking about what an action Agenda for the first 72 hours might look like

title: Idea Generation
description: How do we generate ideas on how to answer the audience's question?

children.text: for a deep-dive into IDEO brainstorming techniques
for an explanation of mind mapping
Gather as much useful content as possible to answer your question (existign content and new analysis)
Run a brainstorming sessioon to generate new ideas and approaches to answering your question - write enverything Download
Select a visual thinking tool to use to keep track of your ideas

title: What is Structure?
description: Techniques to develop ensure clarity through structure

children.text: by John McPhee in the New Yorker
by John McPhee in the New Yorker
A Treatise on God as First Principle
by John Duns Scotus for the first reference to MECE
Create an Arguement Map for your answer to the question
Start by building an inductive arguement that supports your answer to the question
Structure your supporting evidence and ideas into groups of similar ideas, ensuring your groups are MECE and contain no more that 4-5 key ideas

title: Using Stories
description: Using stories to powerfully convey your ideas

children.text: , an extract from a This American Life segment
, an extract from a This American Life segment
Dinstinguishing good anecdotes from relevant ones
The Neuroscience of your Brain on Fiction
by Annie Murphy Pall in the New York Times Sunday Review
Identify how and where you might be able to inject a story into your presentation
Consider adding a story at the beginning of the presentation. in the middle or at the end
Design your story to be a story 'that sticks'

title: Storyboards in Film
description: The origin of storyboarding

children.text: Learn more from this RocketJum Film School
From Word to Image: Storyboarding and the Film Making Process
Start thinking about how you might apply these techniques to your owbn presentation
Complete the next lesson before you start creating your own storyboard
Create your storyboard; write it out on paper
Iterate the storyboard several times, focusing first on key messages, then on flow, and finally on supporting arguments

title: Visual Perception and Communication
description: What are the implications from how people process information?

children.text: Continue to refine your storyboard, now with an emphasis on strikling a balanace between text and visual forms of communication (charts, graphs, diagrams, frameworks)

title: Overview of the Fundamental Elements of Design
description: A brief overview of the fundamentals

title: The Fundamental Elements of Design in Detail
description: Let's go deep

children.text: Play with and learn to use
to learn how to use Google Fonts within PowerPoint
to get up to speed on the alignment guide feature
to learn how to use Google Fonts within PowerPoint
blog post from Dreative Blog for some examples of good font combinations
Use Adobe Color Wheel to create a color palette for your presentation
Select a set of custom fonts to use for your presentation, download and install them in PowerPoint
Review your Storyboard for instances where you are breaking the fundamental principles of design (pay particular attention to whitespace, alignmnet, and proximity)

title: Gestalt Principles of Visual Perception
description: How we can apply principles of visual perception to better communicate our ideas

children.text: Start thining about how you can apply these principles to the design of some of your slides
Take the mock-up of some of your slides in your Storyboard to the next level in terms of detail, applying the Gestalt Principles

title: Slide Composition
description: How to best design slides

children.text: from Ralph Lengler abnd Martin Eppler for inspiration
Start creating your own 'Swipe File' from great McKinsey, BCG, Bain, Oliver Wyman slides
Grab a copy of the SlideHeroes PowerPoint presentation tenmplate
The time is right to move beyond storyboarding and start to design your slides in detail

title: Overview of Fact-based Persuasion
description: Facts and how to present them

children.text: Inspect up close Carles Minard's fantastic
Watch Aaron Koblin's very cool data visualizations in his TED Talk
Play with the New York Time's
interactive graphic of the 2007 Financial Crisis
The Visual Display of Quantitative Information
Start creating your graphs, tables, and charts in PowerPoint
Apply Tufte's principles to your own charts

title: Tables vs. Graphs
description: How to use graphs and tabl;es to present quantitative information

children.text: Determine where you need to display data using graphs, and where tables make more sense

title: Standard Graphs
description: How to properly use graphs to display information

children.text: Watch the supplementary video on YouTube
Read Chapter 3 of the course handbook
Apply the concepts to your project

title: Specialist Graphs
description: Introduction to some common business graphs like the Marimekko and Waterfall

children.text: Watch Hans Rosling's original TED Talk
The Best Stats You've Ever Seen
Journalism in the Age of Data
, a video report on data visualization as a storytelling medium
Read this tutorial for instruction on
How to Make a Merimekko Chart in Excel
Determine what types of relationships you need to illustrate and select your chart type on that basis

title: Preparation and Practice
description: How to prepare for your presentation

children.text: The 10-minute Oral Presentation: What Should I Focus On?
Focus delivery efforst on the 4 key areas discussed in the lesson
Test the length of your presentation and edit to fix alloted time
Practice in front of others and solicit feedback

description: Tips and techniques to improve the delivery of your presentation
